subject: Joint Distributions
topic: §41 Sum of Two Independent Random Variables: Discrete Case
question: Let $X$ and $Y$ be independent random variables each geometrically distributed with parameter $p$, i.e. $p_X(n) = p_Y(n) = \begin{cases} p(1-p)^{n-1} & n = 1, 2, \dots \\ 0 & \text{otherwise.} \end{cases}$ Find the probability mass function of $X+Y$.
explanation: Answer not listed in the book's key — work it through, then check Finan.
